mission.title: Install and Configure CloudNativePG (PostgreSQL Operator)
mission.description: CloudNativePG is an open-source PostgreSQL operator designed to manage PostgreSQL databases within Kubernetes environments, covering the entire operational lifecycle from deployment to maintenance. It is ideal for organizations looking to integrate PostgreSQL management into their Kubernetes workflows.

steps.title: Install CloudNativePG Operator
steps.description:
To install the CloudNativePG operator, use Helm to add the repository and install the operator. Ensure you have Helm v3.5.0 or later installed.

```bash
helm repo add cloudnative-pg https://cloudnative-pg.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --version 1.25.0
```

Verify the installation:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n default
```

steps.title: Create a PostgreSQL Cluster
steps.description:
Create a PostgreSQL cluster by applying a YAML configuration file. Save the following configuration as `postgresql-cluster.yaml`:

```yaml
apiVersion: postgresql.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
spec:
  instances: 3
  storage:
    size: 1Gi
  image: 'docker.io/cloudnativepg/cloudnative-pg:1.25.0'
```

Apply the configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f postgresql-cluster.yaml
```

Check the status of the cluster:
```bash
kubectl get clusters
```

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling for the PostgreSQL cluster. Edit the `Cluster` resource to include autoscaling:

```yaml
spec:
  autoscaling:
    enabled: true
    minReplicas: 3
    maxReplicas: 5
```

Apply the changes:
```bash
kubectl apply -f postgresql-cluster.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall CloudNativePG Operator
uninstall.description:
To uninstall the CloudNativePG operator, first delete the PostgreSQL cluster:
```bash
kubectl delete cluster my-postgres
```
Then uninstall the Helm release:
```bash
helm uninstall cloudnative-pg
```
Finally, clean up any remaining resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace cloudnative-pg
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade CloudNativePG Operator
upgrade.description:
To upgrade the CloudNativePG operator, first backup your PostgreSQL data. Use the following command to create a backup:
```bash
kubectl exec -it <pod-name> -- pg_dumpall -U <username> > backup.sql
```
Check the current version:
```bash
helm list
```
Upgrade the operator using Helm:
```bash
helm upgrade cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --version 1.25.0
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods
```
If issues arise, rollback using:
```bash
helm rollback cloudnative-pg
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a CrashLoopBackOff error, check the logs of the pod:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name>
```
Common causes include insufficient resources or misconfiguration in the `Cluster` spec. Ensure your resource requests and limits are set appropriately.

troubleshooting.title: Cluster Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the PostgreSQL cluster is not starting, check the status of the cluster:
```bash
kubectl describe cluster my-postgres
```
Look for events indicating issues with pod scheduling or resource allocation.

troubleshooting.title: Database Connection Issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot connect to the PostgreSQL database, verify the service is running:
```bash
kubectl get svc
```
Ensure you are using the correct credentials and connection string.

versionNotes.changes: Improved performance for large clusters and added support for automatic backups.
versionNotes.deprecations: The previous backup method using `pg_basebackup` is deprecated in favor of the new automated backup feature.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced enhanced monitoring capabilities and better integration with Kubernetes events.
versionNotes.deprecations: Support for Kubernetes versions below 1.26 has been deprecated.

resolution.summary: A successful setup includes a running CloudNativePG operator and a PostgreSQL cluster that is accessible and properly configured. You should be able to scale the cluster and perform backups without issues.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add cloudnative-pg https://cloudnative-pg.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --version 1.25.0

apiVersion: postgresql.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
spec:
  instances: 3
  storage:
    size: 1Gi
  image: 'docker.io/cloudnativepg/cloudnative-pg:1.25.0'

spec:
  autoscaling:
    enabled: true
    minReplicas: 3
    maxReplicas: 5

prerequisites.cloudCLI: optional cloud CLI tool (gcloud, aws, az, oci)
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or later and have kubectl and Helm installed.
